# Documentation Standards & Conventions

Conventions and best practices for writing documentation that maintains consistency and quality.

## Table of Contents

- [Writing Style](#writing-style)
- [File Organization](#file-organization)
- [Frontmatter](#frontmatter)
- [Code Examples](#code-examples)
- [Cross-References](#cross-references)
- [Versioning](#versioning)
- [Quality Checklist](#quality-checklist)

---

## Writing Style

### Tone & Voice

Documentation should be:
- **Clear** - Easy to understand for target audience
- **Concise** - No unnecessary words or repetition
- **Practical** - Focused on actionable information
- **Inclusive** - Accessible to developers of varying skill levels

**Examples:**

```markdown
// ❌ Too informal
You gotta use pika() to make styles, it's super awesome!

// ✅ Clear and professional
Use pika() to generate atomic CSS classes. Pass style definitions as an object.

// ❌ Too wordy
The pika() function, which is a fundamental part of the PikaCSS system
and is used extensively throughout your project, can be called with
style definitions in the form of an object that contains CSS properties.

// ✅ Concise
Call pika() with a style object to generate atomic CSS classes.
```

### Language Rules

- **English only** - All documentation must be in English
- **Present tense** - "pika() generates CSS" not "pika() will generate CSS"
- **Second person** - "You define styles" instead of "One defines styles"
- **Imperative for instructions** - "Run this command" not "You should run"
- **Active voice** - "PikaCSS generates atomic classes" not "Atomic classes are generated by PikaCSS"

---

## File Organization

### Directory Structure

```
docs/
├── guide/                    # User tutorials and guides
│   ├── basics.md            # Getting started
│   ├── important-concepts.md # Build-time constraint (CANONICAL)
│   ├── configuration.md     # Configuration guide
│   ├── shortcuts.md         # Shortcuts feature
│   └── migration.md         # Migrating from other solutions
│
├── advanced/                # Technical deep-dives
│   ├── typescript.md        # TypeScript setup (CANONICAL)
│   ├── api-reference.md     # Complete API reference
│   ├── plugin-development.md # Creating plugins
│   ├── performance.md       # Performance optimization
│   └── troubleshooting.md   # Common issues and solutions
│
├── integrations/            # Framework and bundler guides
│   ├── index.md            # Overview (CANONICAL)
│   ├── vite.md             # Vite integration
│   ├── nuxt.md             # Nuxt integration
│   ├── webpack.md          # Webpack integration
│   ├── esbuild.md          # Esbuild integration
│   └── rspack.md           # Rspack integration
│
├── plugins/                 # Official plugin documentation
│   ├── icons.md            # Icons plugin
│   ├── reset.md            # Reset plugin
│   └── typography.md       # Typography plugin
│
├── examples/               # Real-world code examples
│   ├── components.md       # Common component patterns
│   └── layouts.md          # Layout patterns
│
├── community/              # Community-focused
│   ├── contributing.md     # Contributing guidelines
│   ├── faq.md             # Frequently asked questions
│   └── changelog.md       # Version history
│
└── llm/                    # LLM-optimized copies
    ├── README.md          # Explain this folder
    ├── guide/            # Mirror of main guides
    ├── advanced/         # Mirror of advanced docs
    └── examples/         # Mirror of examples
```

### File Naming

- Use **kebab-case** for filenames
- Use descriptive names: `plugin-development.md` not `plugins.md`
- Use `.md` extension for Markdown files
- No version numbers in filenames

### File Size Guidelines

- **Too short** - Less than 200 lines (merge with related file or expand)
- **Ideal** - 300-800 lines (focused topic with depth)
- **Too long** - Over 1500 lines (split into multiple files)

---

## Frontmatter

Every documentation file should start with frontmatter:

```markdown
---
title: Feature Name
description: Brief one-sentence description
outline: deep
keywords:
  - keyword1
  - keyword2
---
```

**Fields:**

- `title` - Page title (used in sidebar, browser tab, search)
- `description` - One-sentence description (used in previews)
- `outline` - Table of contents depth (`shallow`, `deep`)
- `keywords` - Search keywords (optional)

**Example:**

```markdown
---
title: TypeScript Configuration
description: Set up TypeScript for full IDE autocomplete and type safety
outline: deep
keywords:
  - typescript
  - types
  - autocomplete
  - configuration
---
```

---

## Code Examples

### Requirements

All code examples must:

- ✅ Be **syntactically correct** (no typos or syntax errors)
- ✅ Be **runnable** (complete, not snippets that won't run)
- ✅ Demonstrate **best practices** (not common mistakes)
- ✅ Use **current API** (version 0.0.39)
- ✅ Include **both good and bad** patterns where teaching a concept

### Syntax Highlighting

```markdown
// Language specified
\`\`\`typescript
const styles = pika({ color: 'red' })
\`\`\`

// For JSX
\`\`\`tsx
function Button() {
  return <button className={pika('btn')}>Click</button>
}
\`\`\`

// For configuration
\`\`\`javascript
// pika.config.ts
export default defineEngineConfig({...})
\`\`\`

// Shell commands
\`\`\`bash
npm install @pikacss/core
\`\`\`
```

### Code Example Patterns

**Good vs Bad Pattern:**

```markdown
// ❌ Bad pattern
\`\`\`typescript
// Don't do this
const styles = pika({ color: props.color })  // Runtime variable!
\`\`\`

// ✅ Good pattern
\`\`\`typescript
// Do this instead
const styles = pika({ color: 'var(--color)' })
<div className={styles} style={{ '--color': props.color }} />
\`\`\`
```

**Inline Code:**

Use backticks for:
- Function names: `pika()`
- Variables: `myStyles`
- Properties: `color: 'red'`
- File names: `pika.config.ts`
- Commands: `npm install`

Don't use backticks for:
- Concepts: "atomic CSS" not `atomic CSS`
- Phrases: "build time" not `build time`

### Testing Examples

Before committing:

1. **Syntax check** - No TypeScript errors
2. **Conceptual check** - Does it teach the concept correctly?
3. **Completeness check** - Can someone copy-paste and run it?
4. **Currency check** - Uses current API version?

---

## Cross-References

### Canonical Sources (Single Source of Truth)

These documents are canonical and should be referenced, not duplicated:

- **`/guide/important-concepts.md`** - Build-time evaluation constraint
- **`/advanced/typescript.md`** - TypeScript setup
- **`/integrations/index.md`** - Integration overview

**Linking pattern:**

```markdown
For more details, see [Important Concepts](/guide/important-concepts.md)
```

### Reference Links

Use relative paths for cross-document links:

```markdown
// Link to file in same directory
[Basics](./basics.md)

// Link to file in subdirectory
[Vite Integration](../integrations/vite.md)

// Link to parent directory
[Guide Home](../../guide/)

// Link with anchor
[Pseudo-classes](#pseudo-classes)

// Deep link
[API Reference - pika() function](/advanced/api-reference.md#pika)
```

### External Links

For external links, specify clearly they're external:

```markdown
// Don't just link without context
[Link](https://example.com)

// Do provide context
Check the [official Vite documentation](https://vitejs.dev) for details.
```

---

## Versioning

### Version Format

- **Patch** (0.0.39 → 0.0.40) - Bug fixes
- **Minor** (0.0.39 → 0.1.0) - New features (backward compatible)
- **Major** (0.0.39 → 1.0.0) - Breaking changes

### Update Locations

When version bumps occur, update:

1. **`package.json`** - Root version field
2. **`AGENTS.md`** - Current Version section
3. **Each skill file** - `metadata.version` in frontmatter
4. **Documentation** - Version-specific references
5. **Examples** - Update API calls if changed

### Version References in Docs

```markdown
// Current version reference
Currently at version 0.0.39

// Version-specific note
Since version 0.0.30

// Breaking change notice
Breaking change in 1.0.0: pika() now requires...
```

---

## Quality Checklist

### Before Committing

- [ ] **Language** - All English, no other languages
- [ ] **Spelling** - Run spell checker or review carefully
- [ ] **Grammar** - Proper sentence structure and punctuation
- [ ] **Links** - All internal links work, external links are clear
- [ ] **Code examples** - Syntax correct, concepts taught properly
- [ ] **Frontmatter** - Present and complete
- [ ] **File size** - Not too short or too long
- [ ] **Organization** - Placed in correct directory
- [ ] **Consistency** - Matches project tone and style
- [ ] **No duplicates** - Not repeating canonical sources

### Build Validation

Before releasing:

```bash
# Build documentation
pnpm docs:build

# Check for build errors
# Look for: errors, warnings, broken links

# Preview locally
pnpm docs:preview

# Manually check:
# - Navigation works
# - Code examples display correctly
# - Links are clickable
# - Formatting looks good
```

### Documentation Tests

Ensure code examples work:

```bash
# For component examples
pnpm --filter example-project build

# For API examples
npm test

# For integration guides
# Manual test with real bundler integration
```

---

## Style Guide

### Headings

Use hierarchical heading structure:

```markdown
# Main Page Title (h1 - only one per page)

## Major Section (h2)

### Subsection (h3)

#### Minor Point (h4)
```

### Lists

Use unordered lists for non-sequential items:

```markdown
- Point 1
- Point 2
- Point 3
```

Use ordered lists for sequential steps:

```markdown
1. First step
2. Second step
3. Third step
```

### Emphasis

Use markdown emphasis appropriately:

- **Bold** for important concepts: `**atomic CSS**`
- _Italic_ for emphasis: `_especially important_`
- `Code` for technical terms: `` `pika()` ``

### Blockquotes

Use for important warnings or notes:

```markdown
> ⚠️ **Important**: All `pika()` arguments must be statically analyzable.
```

---

## Terminology Consistency

Always use consistent terminology:

| Term | Definition | Example |
|------|-----------|---------|
| **atomic CSS** | One CSS property per class | `.a { color: red; }` |
| **shortcut** | Pre-defined style combination | `pika('btn-primary')` |
| **build time** | During project build | `pika()` calls evaluated here |
| **runtime** | During app execution | Props, state, user input |
| **preflights** | Global styles injected first | Reset, base styles |
| **selector `$`** | Reference to current element | `'$:hover'`, `'$ > span'` |
| **engine** | Core PikaCSS processor | Transforms style definitions |
| **plugin** | Extends engine functionality | Icons, Reset, Typography |
| **variant** | Pre-defined style set | `btn-primary`, `btn-secondary` |

---

## Common Abbreviations

Use consistently:

- **API** - Application Programming Interface
- **CSS** - Cascading Style Sheets
- **IDE** - Integrated Development Environment
- **NPM** - Node Package Manager
- **CLI** - Command Line Interface
- **HMR** - Hot Module Replacement
- **JSX** - JavaScript XML syntax

---

## Footnotes & References

For additional context use footnotes:

```markdown
This feature[^1] allows dynamic styling.

[^1]: All values must be statically evaluable at build time.
```

Or use info boxes:

```markdown
> 💡 **Tip**: Use shortcuts for frequently used styles to reduce CSS size.
```

---

## Accessibility

Write with accessibility in mind:

- Provide alt text for images
- Use semantic heading hierarchy
- Don't rely solely on color for meaning
- Include text alternatives for icons
- Use clear, simple language

---

## SEO Considerations

Help documentation be discoverable:

- Use descriptive page titles
- Write meaningful page descriptions (frontmatter)
- Use keywords naturally
- Structure with clear headings
- Include links to related pages
- Keep URLs descriptive and short

---

## Format Examples

### Feature Documentation Template

```markdown
---
title: Feature Name
description: Brief description
outline: deep
---

# Feature Name

Brief intro (1-2 sentences explaining what this is)

## Overview

What the feature does and when to use it

## Basic Usage

Simplest example

## Advanced Usage

More complex patterns

## Configuration Options

All configurable options with examples

## Best Practices

- Practice 1
- Practice 2

## Common Issues

| Problem | Solution |
|---------|----------|
| Issue 1 | Solution 1 |

## Related Topics

- Link to related feature
- Link to reference docs
```

### API Reference Template

```markdown
# Function/Class Name

Brief description

## Syntax

\`\`\`typescript
function name(param: Type): ReturnType
\`\`\`

## Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| param1 | string | Yes | Description |

## Returns

Description of return value

## Examples

\`\`\`typescript
// Example 1
// Example 2
\`\`\`

## See Also

- Related function
- Related concept
```

---

## Maintenance Workflow

When updating documentation:

1. **Identify** - Which file needs updating
2. **Edit** - Make changes following these standards
3. **Review** - Check against quality checklist
4. **Test** - Build and preview locally
5. **Commit** - With clear commit message
6. **Deploy** - After review and approval
